mnemonics.title: D for Dependent - D for Determinant = 0
mnemonics.content: When the determinant of the matrix formed by vectors is ZERO → Vectors are DEPENDENT. Non-zero determinant → INDEPENDENT.

mnemonics.title: L-I-D-E to Remember Independence
mnemonics.content: L - Linear I - Independence D - Depends on E - Essential coefficients being ALL ZERO. If only trivial solution → Independent!

mnemonics.title: Count Rule for Independence
mnemonics.content: In Rⁿ, you can have AT MOST n linearly independent vectors. More than n → Definitely dependent!

mnemonics.title: Zero is the Hero of Dependence
mnemonics.content: Zero vector in any set = automatic dependence. Remember: ZERO ruins independence!

acronyms.expansion: Linear Combination Sum - c1v1 + c2v2 + ... + ckvk
acronyms.usage: To check dependence/independence, set this equal to zero vector and solve.
Transform matrix to RREF; zero rows indicate dependent vectors.
If NTS exists for homogeneous system → Dependent. Only trivial solution → Independent.

visualTips.visualization: Think of two arrows from origin. If they point in different directions (not parallel), they are independent. If they overlap or are collinear, they are dependent.
Three arrows. They are independent if they form the edges of a 3D box (no flat collapse). Dependent if they all lie in one plane or line.
Picture rows as lines. In RREF, each leading 1 (pivot) is like a 'support pillar'. If you lose a pillar (zero row), the structure collapses → dependent.
In R², det = 0 means parallelogram collapses to line (zero area). In R³, det = 0 means parallelepiped collapses to flat shape (zero volume). Both indicate dependence.

analogies.analogy: Like having duplicate keys. If you have keys A, B, C where C = A + B, then C is redundant. You only need A and B to open the same doors.
Like independent directions on a compass. North-South and East-West give you 4 directions. Each adds new 'information' that can't be derived from others.
Two independent vectors in R² are like two perpendicular ropes that can stretch to cover an entire floor. Dependent vectors are like parallel ropes - they only cover one direction.
In a 2D room, you can't have 3 perpendicular walls meeting at corners. Similarly, in R² you can't have 3 independent directions.
